title: Using tters with TrialEmulation
text: Rust toolchain needed install binary build r-universe.
code: install.packages("tters",   repos = c("https://oldschoolcool2.r-universe.dev", "https://cloud.r-project.org"))

what: Standalone: expand a cohort in memory
title: Using tters with TrialEmulation
text: *_df functions take data.frame return one, intermediate files. Column dtypes preserved exactly (including 64-bit bit64::integer64 ids). Parquet-/ Parquet-path (expand_trial()), pre-computed-weight (expand_trial_weighted()) -Rust weight-fitting (fit_trial_weights(), expand_trial_weighted_fitted()) variants exist — see help pages package reference.
code: library(tters)  # long person-time: one row per (id, period) cohort <- data.frame(   id        = c(1L, 1L, 1L, 2L, 2L),   period    = c(0L, 1L, 2L, 0L, 1L),   treatment = c(1L, 1L, 0L, 0L, 1L),   eligible  = c(1L, 0L, 0L, 1L, 0L),   outcome   = c(0L, 0L, 1L, 0L, 0L) )  expanded <- expand_trial_df(cohort, estimand = "ITT") head(expanded)

title: Using tters with TrialEmulation
text: Set trial_sequence() exactly TrialEmulation::expand_trials(), run expansion Rust pointing output save_to_tters() calling expand_trials_tters(). Everything downstream — sampling, fit_msm() — unchanged bit-identical. produced frame byte-equivalent TrialEmulation::expand_trials() (structural columns bit-exact, weight within machine precision), load_expanded_data(), sample_controls(), fit_msm() behave identically. TrialEmulation Rust build unavailable — -yet- supported “treated” estimand — expand_trials_tters() falls back TrialEmulation::expand_trials() message.
code: library(TrialEmulation) library(tters) data("data_censored")  trial <- trial_sequence("ITT") |>   set_data(data = data_censored) |>   set_censor_weight_model(     censor_event = "censored", numerator = ~x2, denominator = ~ x2 + x1,     pool_models = "numerator",     model_fitter = stats_glm_logit(save_path = tempfile())   ) |>   calculate_weights() |>                    # weight MODELS fit in R   set_outcome_model(adjustment_terms = ~x2) |>   set_expansion_options(output = save_to_tters(), chunk_size = 0)  trial <- expand_trials_tters(trial)         # the EXPANSION runs in Rust trial <- load_expanded_data(trial, seed = 1234, p_control = 0.5) trial <- fit_msm(trial)                     # estimation stays in R

title: Authors and Citation
text: Michael Batech. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation

what: Sequential Target Trial Emulation Data Expansion (Rust + Polars Backend)
title: Sequential Target Trial Emulation Data Expansion (Rust + Polars Backend)
text: tters R companion package exposes tte-expand Rust core crate R via extendr. reproduces, bit--bit, sequential trial-emulation data expansion TrialEmulation R package, Parquet path, -memory data.frame path, TrialEmulation companion backend (see ../../ROADMAP.md).

title: Sequential Target Trial Emulation Data Expansion (Rust + Polars Backend)
text: Rust toolchain required install r-universe (binary builds provided). Building source needs Cargo / rustc >= 1.95.
code: install.packages("tters",   repos = c("https://oldschoolcool2.r-universe.dev", "https://cloud.r-project.org"))

title: Sequential Target Trial Emulation Data Expansion (Rust + Polars Backend)
text: Rust crate src/rust/ declares empty [workspace] table, making workspace root Cargo.lock. required R CMD INSTALL builds self-contained, reproducible (CRAN-vendorable) crate. excluded repo-root workspace (exclude = ["bindings/tters"] root Cargo.toml) therefore linted main CI --workspace clippy job — extendr’s macro-expanded FFI otherwise trip pedantic lints.
code: bindings/tters/ ├── DESCRIPTION, NAMESPACE          # R package metadata ├── R/                              # R wrappers (extendr-wrappers.R is generated) ├── configure[.win], cleanup[.win]  # R CMD INSTALL build hooks ├── tools/                          # config.R / msrv.R (toolchain checks) └── src/     ├── Makevars.in, Makevars.win.in, entrypoint.c, tters-win.def     └── rust/                       # a DETACHED Cargo workspace         ├── Cargo.toml              # extendr-api + path dep on ../../../../crates/tte-expand         └── src/lib.rs              # #[extendr] FFI shims

title: Sequential Target Trial Emulation Data Expansion (Rust + Polars Backend)
text: tters plugs upstream TrialEmulation’s te_datastore extension API trial_sequence() pipeline runs expensive expansion Rust estimation (sampling + marginal structural model) stays R, consuming Rust output bit-identically default path. Suggests-level opt-: TrialEmulation never Imports, backend -op anyone call . Validated TrialEmulation v0.0.4.11. Set trial sequence exactly TrialEmulation::expand_trials(), call expand_trials_tters() instead — everything downstream unchanged: produced frame byte-equivalent TrialEmulation::expand_trials() (structural columns bit-exact, weight within machine precision), load_expanded_data(), sample_controls(), fit_msm() behave identically. split deliberate: Rust owns deterministic data transformation; R owns statistical estimation. per-period weight wt computed calculate_weights() read verbatim — Rust performs deterministic accumulation. TrialEmulation (Rust build) unavailable, -yet-supported estimand, expand_trials_tters() falls back TrialEmulation::expand_trials() message (fallback = FALSE force Rust path).
code: library(TrialEmulation) library(tters) data("data_censored")  trial <- trial_sequence("ITT") |>   set_data(data = data_censored) |>   set_censor_weight_model(     censor_event = "censored", numerator = ~x2, denominator = ~ x2 + x1,     pool_models = "numerator",     model_fitter = stats_glm_logit(save_path = tempfile())   ) |>   calculate_weights() |>                       # weight MODELS fit in R   set_outcome_model(adjustment_terms = ~x2) |>   set_expansion_options(output = save_to_tters(), chunk_size = 0)  trial <- expand_trials_tters(trial)            # the EXPANSION runs in Rust trial <- load_expanded_data(trial, seed = 1234, p_control = 0.5) trial <- fit_msm(trial)                        # estimation stays in R

title: Sequential Target Trial Emulation Data Expansion (Rust + Polars Backend)
text: files mirror rextendr produces. (re)scaffold refresh changing Rust signatures, directory R: Verify pinned versions relying scaffold. extendr-api pinned 0.9 Config/rextendr/version 0.5.0 research confirmed crates.io offline. Run rextendr::document() cargo update (network) reconcile installed versions.
code: # install.packages("rextendr") rextendr::document()        # regenerates R/extendr-wrappers.R + NAMESPACE # or, to (re)create the binding scaffold from scratch: # rextendr::use_extendr()

title: Sequential Target Trial Emulation Data Expansion (Rust + Polars Backend)
text: Path dependency vs. CRAN. cargo vendor vendor path dependencies, R CMD build tarballs files bindings/tters/. CRAN submission, either publish tte-expand registry switch version dependency, add build step copies crates/tte-expand/ package. vendored Polars tree also large; r-universe / source install realistic channel. Two lockfiles. Commit root Cargo.lock src/rust/Cargo.lock (Dependabot tracks latter /bindings/tters/src/rust).

what: Expand an in-memory cohort data.frame into the sequential target-trial layout and return the result as a data.frame — the frame-in/frame-out analogue of expand_parquet(), with no intermediate Parquet. — expand_df
title: Expand an in-memory cohort data.frame into the sequential target-trial layout and return the result as a data.frame — the frame-in/frame-out analogue of expand_parquet(), with no intermediate Parquet. — expand_df
text: cohort arrives R data.frame (list equal-length columns); columns marshalled dtype-exactly Polars frame (R integer -> Int32, double -> Float64, bit64::integer64 -> Int64), expanded verified core, six structural columns marshalled back R data.frame. 64-bit integer column (integer64, e.g. large id) round-trips exactly via pure-safe bit reinterpret (precision loss).

title: Expand an in-memory cohort data.frame into the sequential target-trial layout and return the result as a data.frame — the frame-in/frame-out analogue of expand_parquet(), with no intermediate Parquet. — expand_df
code: expand_df(   cohort,   id_col,   period_col,   treatment_col,   eligible_col,   outcome_col,   first_period,   last_period,   estimand )

title: Expand an in-memory cohort data.frame into the sequential target-trial layout and return the result as a data.frame — the frame-in/frame-out analogue of expand_parquet(), with no intermediate Parquet. — expand_df
text: cohort R data.frame long person-time rows. id_col, period_col, treatment_col Column names cohort. eligible_col, outcome_col Eligibility / outcome column names. first_period, last_period Inclusive integer bounds trial_period. estimand "ITT" "PP". Case-insensitive.

what: Expand a prepared person-time Parquet dataset into the sequential target-trial layout and write the result to output_path. — expand_parquet
title: Expand a prepared person-time Parquet dataset into the sequential target-trial layout and write the result to output_path. — expand_parquet
text: thin FFI shim. dtype-exact, deterministic Polars work lives tte_expand core crate (#![forbid(unsafe_code)]). binding crate forbid unsafe extendr macros emit FFI registrar. Every tte_expand::ExpandError mapped R error condition.

title: Expand a prepared person-time Parquet dataset into the sequential target-trial layout and write the result to output_path. — expand_parquet
code: expand_parquet(   input_path,   output_path,   id_col,   period_col,   treatment_col,   eligible_col,   outcome_col,   first_period,   last_period,   estimand )

title: Expand a prepared person-time Parquet dataset into the sequential target-trial layout and write the result to output_path. — expand_parquet
text: input_path Path input Parquet file. output_path Path expanded Parquet written. id_col, period_col, treatment_col Column names input. eligible_col, outcome_col Eligibility / outcome column names (TrialEmulation defaults "eligible" / "outcome"). first_period, last_period Inclusive integer bounds trial_period. estimand "ITT" (artificial censoring) "PP" (per-protocol, censor trial first treatment deviation). Case-insensitive.

title: Expand a prepared person-time Parquet dataset into the sequential target-trial layout and write the result to output_path. — expand_parquet
text: NULL, invisibly; expansion written output_path. Errors core engine surface R errors.

Defaults match TrialEmulation conventions. first_period, last_period Inclusive integer period bounds. estimand "ITT" (intention--treat, artificial censoring) "PP" (per-protocol, censor trial first treatment deviation).

what: Expand a target-trial cohort data.frame in memory (ergonomic wrapper) — expand_trial_df
title: Expand a target-trial cohort data.frame in memory (ergonomic wrapper) — expand_trial_df
text: Frame-/ frame-analogue expand_trial(): takes -memory cohort data.frame returns expanded trial frame data.frame, intermediate Parquet. Wraps extendr-generated expand_df().

title: Expand a target-trial cohort data.frame in memory (ergonomic wrapper) — expand_trial_df
code: expand_trial_df(   cohort,   id_col = "id",   period_col = "period",   treatment_col = "treatment",   eligible_col = "eligible",   outcome_col = "outcome",   first_period = 0L,   last_period = .Machine$integer.max,   estimand = "ITT" )

title: Expand a target-trial cohort data.frame in memory (ergonomic wrapper) — expand_trial_df
text: cohort data.frame (tibble / data.table / Arrow Table) long person-time rows. Coerced .data.frame(), preserves integer64 column's class. id_col, period_col, treatment_col, eligible_col, outcome_col Column names. Defaults match TrialEmulation conventions. first_period, last_period Inclusive integer period bounds. estimand "ITT" (intention--treat, artificial censoring) "PP" (per-protocol, censor trial first treatment deviation).

title: Expand a target-trial cohort data.frame in memory (ergonomic wrapper) — expand_trial_df
text: data.frame six structural columns (id, trial_period, followup_time, assigned_treatment, treatment, outcome); integer64 input column returned integer64.

title: Expand a target-trial cohort data.frame in memory (ergonomic wrapper) — expand_trial_df
text: Column dtypes preserved exactly: R integer <-> Polars Int32, double <-> Float64, bit64::integer64 <-> Int64. 64-bit integer column (e.g. large id) round-trips exactly integer64 precision loss 2^53 (pure-safe bit reinterpret, numeric cast).

title: Expand a target-trial cohort data.frame in memory (ergonomic wrapper) — expand_trial_df
code: cohort <- data.frame(   id = c(1L, 1L, 1L, 2L, 2L), period = c(0L, 1L, 2L, 0L, 1L),   treatment = c(1L, 1L, 0L, 0L, 1L), eligible = c(1L, 0L, 0L, 1L, 0L),   outcome = c(0L, 0L, 1L, 0L, 0L) ) expand_trial_df(cohort, estimand = "ITT") #>   id trial_period followup_time assigned_treatment treatment outcome #> 1  1            0             0                  1         1       0 #> 2  1            0             1                  1         1       0 #> 3  1            0             2                  1         0       1 #> 4  2            0             0                  0         0       0 #> 5  2            0             1                  0         1       0

what: Expand a dataset and attach pre-computed inverse-probability weights — expand_trial_weighted
title: Expand a dataset and attach pre-computed inverse-probability weights — expand_trial_weighted
text: User-facing wrapper around extendr-generated expand_weighted_parquet(). expands input_path estimand, joins per-(id, period) factor table factors_path (id, period, weight_factor), writes six structural columns plus cumulative-product weight. Weight values produced upstream R; engine reproduces deterministic accumulation.

title: Expand a dataset and attach pre-computed inverse-probability weights — expand_trial_weighted
code: expand_trial_weighted(   input_path,   factors_path,   output_path,   id_col = "id",   period_col = "period",   treatment_col = "treatment",   eligible_col = "eligible",   outcome_col = "outcome",   first_period = 0L,   last_period = .Machine$integer.max,   estimand = "PP" )

title: Expand a dataset and attach pre-computed inverse-probability weights — expand_trial_weighted
text: input_path Path existing input Parquet file. factors_path Path per-(id, period) factor Parquet file. output_path Path write weighted Parquet file. id_col, period_col, treatment_col, eligible_col, outcome_col Column names. Defaults match TrialEmulation conventions. first_period, last_period Inclusive integer period bounds. estimand "ITT" "PP"; selects weight model upstream, application arithmetic identical .

title: Expand a dataset and attach pre-computed inverse-probability weights — expand_trial_weighted
text: output_path, invisibly.

title: Expand a dataset and attach pre-computed inverse-probability weights — expand_trial_weighted
code: input <- system.file("extdata", "weights", "input_data_censored.parquet",                      package = "tters") factors <- system.file("extdata", "weights",                        "input_data_censored_itt_weights.parquet",                        package = "tters") expand_trial_weighted(input, factors, tempfile(fileext = ".parquet"),                       estimand = "ITT")

code: expand_trial_weighted_df(   cohort,   factors,   id_col = "id",   period_col = "period",   treatment_col = "treatment",   eligible_col = "eligible",   outcome_col = "outcome",   first_period = 0L,   last_period = .Machine$integer.max,   estimand = "PP" )

title: Expand a cohort data.frame and attach pre-computed weights, in memory (wrapper) — expand_trial_weighted_df
text: cohort data.frame (tibble / data.table / Arrow Table) long person-time rows. Coerced .data.frame(). factors data.frame columns id, period, weight_factor. Coerced .data.frame(). id_col, period_col, treatment_col, eligible_col, outcome_col Column names. Defaults match TrialEmulation conventions. first_period, last_period Inclusive integer period bounds. estimand "ITT" "PP"; selects weight model upstream, application arithmetic identical .

title: Expand a cohort data.frame and attach pre-computed weights, in memory (wrapper) — expand_trial_weighted_df
text: data.frame six structural columns plus weight.

title: Expand a cohort data.frame and attach pre-computed weights, in memory (wrapper) — expand_trial_weighted_df
code: cohort <- data.frame(   id = c(1L, 1L, 1L, 2L, 2L), period = c(0L, 1L, 2L, 0L, 1L),   treatment = c(1L, 1L, 0L, 0L, 1L), eligible = c(1L, 0L, 0L, 1L, 0L),   outcome = c(0L, 0L, 1L, 0L, 0L) ) factors <- data.frame(   id = c(1L, 1L, 1L, 2L, 2L), period = c(0L, 1L, 2L, 0L, 1L),   weight_factor = c(1, 0.9, 1.05, 1, 1.1) ) expand_trial_weighted_df(cohort, factors, estimand = "PP") #>   id trial_period followup_time assigned_treatment treatment outcome weight #> 1  1            0             0                  1         1       0    1.0 #> 2  1            0             1                  1         1       0    0.9 #> 3  2            0             0                  0         0       0    1.0

what: Fit IPW weights and expand a cohort into a weighted trial frame (ergonomic wrapper) — expand_trial_weighted_fitted
title: Fit IPW weights and expand a cohort into a weighted trial frame (ergonomic wrapper) — expand_trial_weighted_fitted
text: User-facing wrapper around extendr-generated expand_weighted_fitted_parquet(). takes raw person-time cohort straight weighted, expanded trial frame one call — fitting switching /IPCW models Rust (pre-computed factor table), expanding estimand, accumulating fitted factor cumulative weight. six structural columns bit-exact; weight matches Oracle within staged ~1e-6 tolerance. Robust/sandwich variance marginal structural model stay R.

title: Fit IPW weights and expand a cohort into a weighted trial frame (ergonomic wrapper) — expand_trial_weighted_fitted
code: expand_trial_weighted_fitted(   input_path,   output_path,   id_col = "id",   period_col = "period",   treatment_col = "treatment",   eligible_col = "eligible",   outcome_col = "outcome",   first_period = 0L,   last_period = .Machine$integer.max,   estimand = "PP",   switch_numerator = NULL,   switch_denominator = NULL,   censor_col = NULL,   censor_numerator = NULL,   censor_denominator = NULL,   pool_censor = "none" )

title: Fit IPW weights and expand a cohort into a weighted trial frame (ergonomic wrapper) — expand_trial_weighted_fitted
text: input_path Path existing input Parquet cohort (long person-time). output_path Path write weighted, expanded Parquet. id_col, period_col, treatment_col, eligible_col, outcome_col Column names. Defaults match TrialEmulation conventions. first_period, last_period Inclusive integer period bounds. estimand "ITT" "PP". Per-protocol runs artificial-censoring state machine switching models; intention--treat skips . switch_numerator, switch_denominator Character vectors covariate column names switching numerator (stabiliser) / denominator models, NULL (default) omit switching weights. censor_col Name {0,1} censoring-indicator column; modelled response 1 - censor_col. NULL (default) omits IPCW weights. censor_numerator, censor_denominator Character vectors covariate column names IPCW numerator / denominator models. pool_censor IPCW models pooled across previous-treatment strata: "none", "numerator", "".

title: Fit IPW weights and expand a cohort into a weighted trial frame (ergonomic wrapper) — expand_trial_weighted_fitted
text: output_path, invisibly.

title: Fit IPW weights and expand a cohort into a weighted trial frame (ergonomic wrapper) — expand_trial_weighted_fitted
text: Model presence follows rule fit_trial_weights(): switching model fitted either switch_* covariate vector non-NULL; IPCW model fitted censor_col non-NULL.

title: Fit IPW weights and expand a cohort into a weighted trial frame (ergonomic wrapper) — expand_trial_weighted_fitted
code: # Raw cohort straight to a weighted, expanded frame in one call: input <- system.file("extdata", "weights", "input_data_censored.parquet",                      package = "tters") expand_trial_weighted_fitted(input, tempfile(fileext = ".parquet"),                              estimand = "PP", switch_numerator = "x2",                              switch_denominator = c("x2", "x1"))

NULL omits IPCW weights. censor_numerator, censor_denominator Character vectors covariate column names IPCW numerator / denominator models. pool_censor IPCW models pooled across previous-treatment strata: "none", "numerator", "".

title: Fit IPW weights and expand a cohort data.frame in one call, in memory (wrapper) — expand_trial_weighted_fitted_df
code: if (requireNamespace("arrow", quietly = TRUE)) {   cohort <- as.data.frame(arrow::read_parquet(system.file(     "extdata", "weights", "input_data_censored.parquet", package = "tters")))   weighted <- expand_trial_weighted_fitted_df(cohort, estimand = "PP",     switch_numerator = "x2", switch_denominator = c("x2", "x1")) }

what: Expand a sequence of target trials with the Rust + Polars engine — expand_trials_tters
title: Expand a sequence of target trials with the Rust + Polars engine — expand_trials_tters
text: drop-replacement TrialEmulation::expand_trials() runs expensive expansion Rust (tters) instead R, stores result trial_sequence's registered te_datastore. produced frame byte-equivalent default path (structural columns bit-exact, weight within machine precision), downstream — load_expanded_data(), sample_controls(), fit_msm() — behaves identically.

title: Expand a sequence of target trials with the Rust + Polars engine — expand_trials_tters
code: expand_trials_tters(object, fallback = TRUE, quiet = FALSE)

title: Expand a sequence of target trials with the Rust + Polars engine — expand_trials_tters
text: object configured trial_sequence (ITT PP). estimand yet supported falls back R. fallback TRUE (default), failure Rust path (including unsupported estimand missing toolchain) falls back TrialEmulation::expand_trials() message. FALSE, error raised. quiet TRUE, suppress fallback message.

title: Expand a sequence of target trials with the Rust + Polars engine — expand_trials_tters
text: updated trial_sequence, @expansion@datastore populated — object type TrialEmulation::expand_trials() returns.

title: Expand a sequence of target trials with the Rust + Polars engine — expand_trials_tters
text: Estimation stays entirely R. Weight models fit calculate_weights(); function reads per-period wt verbatim Rust performs deterministic expansion weight accumulation. Set trial_sequence exactly TrialEmulation::expand_trials() (set_data() -> optional weight models + calculate_weights() -> set_outcome_model() -> set_expansion_options()), call instead expand_trials(). registered output may save_to_tters() te_datastore (e.g. save_to_datatable()); speedup comes Rust expansion, store.

title: Expand a sequence of target trials with the Rust + Polars engine — expand_trials_tters
code: # \donttest{ if (requireNamespace("TrialEmulation", quietly = TRUE)) {   library(TrialEmulation)   data("data_censored")   trial <- trial_sequence("ITT") |>     set_data(data = data_censored) |>     set_censor_weight_model(       censor_event = "censored", numerator = ~x2, denominator = ~ x2 + x1,       pool_models = "numerator",       model_fitter = stats_glm_logit(save_path = tempfile())     ) |>     calculate_weights() |>     set_outcome_model(adjustment_terms = ~x2) |>     set_expansion_options(output = save_to_tters(), chunk_size = 0)   trial <- expand_trials_tters(trial)   load_expanded_data(trial, seed = 1234, p_control = 0.5) } # }

what: Fit the IPW weights in Rust, expand the cohort, apply the weights, and write the weighted trial frame — a raw cohort to a weighted, expanded frame in one call (no pre-computed factor table). — expand_weighted_fitted_parquet
title: Fit the IPW weights in Rust, expand the cohort, apply the weights, and write the weighted trial frame — a raw cohort to a weighted, expanded frame in one call (no pre-computed factor table). — expand_weighted_fitted_parquet
text: thin FFI shim tte_expand::expand_weighted_fitted_parquet: fully -Rust analogue expand_weighted_parquet(). fits switching /IPCW models spec (fit_weights_parquet() ), expands estimand, joins accumulates fitted factor, writes six structural columns plus cumulative-product weight. Structural columns bit-exact; weight matches Oracle within staged ~1e-6 tolerance (ADR-2).

what: Fit inverse-probability weights for a target-trial cohort (ergonomic wrapper) — fit_trial_weights
title: Fit inverse-probability weights for a target-trial cohort (ergonomic wrapper) — fit_trial_weights
text: User-facing wrapper around extendr-generated fit_weights_parquet() fits IPW switching /IPCW censoring models Rust writes per-(id, period) factor table (id, period, weight_factor) — table expand_trial_weighted() consumes. Unlike pre-computed-factor path, weight models fitted Rust (weights-fit surface): faithful port TrialEmulation's design preparation plus deterministic binomial-logit solver. Robust/sandwich variance marginal structural model stay R.

title: Fit inverse-probability weights for a target-trial cohort (ergonomic wrapper) — fit_trial_weights
code: fit_trial_weights(   input_path,   output_path,   id_col = "id",   period_col = "period",   treatment_col = "treatment",   eligible_col = "eligible",   outcome_col = "outcome",   first_period = 0L,   last_period = .Machine$integer.max,   estimand = "PP",   switch_numerator = NULL,   switch_denominator = NULL,   censor_col = NULL,   censor_numerator = NULL,   censor_denominator = NULL,   pool_censor = "none" )

title: Fit inverse-probability weights for a target-trial cohort (ergonomic wrapper) — fit_trial_weights
text: input_path Path existing input Parquet cohort (long person-time). output_path Path write (id, period, weight_factor) Parquet. id_col, period_col, treatment_col, eligible_col, outcome_col Column names. Defaults match TrialEmulation conventions. first_period, last_period Inclusive integer period bounds. estimand "ITT" "PP". Per-protocol runs artificial-censoring state machine switching models; intention--treat skips . switch_numerator, switch_denominator Character vectors covariate column names switching numerator (stabiliser) / denominator models, NULL (default) omit switching weights. censor_col Name {0,1} censoring-indicator column; modelled response 1 - censor_col. NULL (default) omits IPCW weights. censor_numerator, censor_denominator Character vectors covariate column names IPCW numerator / denominator models. pool_censor IPCW models pooled across previous-treatment strata: "none", "numerator", "".

title: Fit inverse-probability weights for a target-trial cohort (ergonomic wrapper) — fit_trial_weights
text: output_path, invisibly.

title: Fit inverse-probability weights for a target-trial cohort (ergonomic wrapper) — fit_trial_weights
text: switching model fitted either switch_numerator switch_denominator non-NULL; IPCW censoring model fitted censor_col non-NULL. Covariates character vectors column names; character(0) (NULL) yields intercept-model.

title: Fit inverse-probability weights for a target-trial cohort (ergonomic wrapper) — fit_trial_weights
code: # Per-protocol switching weights (numerator ~ x2, denominator ~ x2 + x1): input <- system.file("extdata", "weights", "input_data_censored.parquet",                      package = "tters") fit_trial_weights(input, tempfile(fileext = ".parquet"), estimand = "PP",                   switch_numerator = "x2", switch_denominator = c("x2", "x1"))

title: Fit inverse-probability weights for a cohort data.frame, in memory (wrapper) — fit_trial_weights_df
code: # Fitting needs covariates, so load the shipped `data_censored` cohort: if (requireNamespace("arrow", quietly = TRUE)) {   cohort <- as.data.frame(arrow::read_parquet(system.file(     "extdata", "weights", "input_data_censored.parquet", package = "tters")))   factors <- fit_trial_weights_df(cohort, estimand = "PP",     switch_numerator = "x2", switch_denominator = c("x2", "x1")) }

what: Fit the inverse-probability weight factor for a Parquet cohort in Rust and write the per-(id, period) factor table (id, period, weight_factor). — fit_weights_parquet
title: Fit the inverse-probability weight factor for a Parquet cohort in Rust and write the per-(id, period) factor table (id, period, weight_factor). — fit_weights_parquet
text: thin FFI shim tte_expand::fit_weights_parquet (weights-fit surface). Unlike expand_weighted_parquet(), applies pre-computed factor table, fits IPW models Rust: ports TrialEmulation's data_manipulation + censor_func design preparation binds deterministic binomial-logit solver switching /IPCW censoring models, forms wt = wt_switch * wtC. structural design exact; fitted factors reproduce R glm within staged ~1e-6 tolerance (ADR-2), bit--bit. Robust/sandwich variance marginal structural model stay R.

title: Create a te_datastore_tters storage backend — save_to_tters
code: # The storage backend on its own: save_to_tters() #> Error: save_to_tters() requires the 'TrialEmulation' and 'data.table' packages. Install them with install.packages(c('TrialEmulation', 'data.table')).  # Wired into a trial_sequence() pipeline (requires TrialEmulation): # \donttest{ if (requireNamespace("TrialEmulation", quietly = TRUE)) {   library(TrialEmulation)   data("data_censored")   trial_sequence("ITT") |>     set_data(data = data_censored) |>     set_outcome_model(adjustment_terms = ~x2) |>     set_expansion_options(output = save_to_tters(), chunk_size = 0) } # }

what: tters: Sequential Target Trial Emulation Data Expansion — tters-package

title: tters: Sequential Target Trial Emulation Data Expansion — tters-package
text: Maintainer: Michael Batech oldschoolcool@gmail.com (ORCID) Authors: Michael Batech oldschoolcool@gmail.com (ORCID)

title: tters 0.1.1
text: Badge-green maintenance release tters R binding (engine changes). ERROR fixed: bare read_expanded_data() calls te_datastore contract test now resolve via TrialEmulation:: — R CMD check runs testthat TrialEmulation installed---attached, every non-WASM r-universe platform job previously failed (#21). WARNING fixed: methods utils declared Imports, matching importFrom() methods, bit64::integer64 (declaration — exact int64 round-trip untouched), utils::globalVariables. NOTE fixed: globalVariables(c(":=", "id")) data.table NSE symbols. R CMD check built tarball: 0 errors, 0 warnings, 0 notes.

title: tters 0.1.0
text: First public release tters, verified Rust + Polars backend data-expansion stage sequential target trial emulation, bit-exact R TrialEmulation Oracle (see report/certificate.md). Sequential trial expansion (ITT per-protocol) dtype-exact, deterministic output. Pre-computed IPW application -Rust weight fitting (weights-fit). -memory data.frame round-trip, including exact bit64::integer64 columns. te_datastore companion backend upstream TrialEmulation pipelines.
